title: Rootless container: Cannot write to tmpfs after stop and start of container
body:
### Issue Description

Inside the container, I can write to `/app/temp` which is mounted with `--tmpfs`. But when I stop the container and then start it again, I get "Permission denied" when I try to write there.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

## Dockerfile

```
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1

FROM python:3.11-slim
ENV TEMP=/app/temp
ENV TMP=/app/temp
USER root
RUN useradd lrs
RUN mkdir    /app /app/work && \
    chown lrs:lrs /app/work
USER lrs
WORKDIR /app/work
CMD [ "/usr/bin/sleep", "1d" ]
```

## Build it and first test
```
podman build -f Dockerfile --tag test-sleep
podman run -d --mount type=tmpfs,destination=/app/temp --name test-sleep test-sleep
podman exec -it test-sleep bash
ls -l ..
touch /app/temp/test.txt
exit
```
Works as expected, `/app/temp` shown as `drwxrwxrwt. 2 root root 40 Feb 14 15:24 temp`

## Test again after stop and start
```
podman stop test-sleep
podman start test-sleep
podman exec -it test-sleep bash
ls -l ..
touch /app/temp/test.txt
exit
```

### Describe the results you received

Now we get "Permission denied" error, and `/app/temp` shown as `drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 40 Feb 14 15:26 temp`.

### Describe the results you expected

Writing to `/app/temp` should just work after `podman stop` and `podman start` as in the first test.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

The test fails on RHEL 8.8 with Podman 4.4.1.
The same test works on Ubuntu 22.04 with Podman 3.4.4.
Furthermore, the test works with Docker on SLES 12 SP5.

comments.body:
This seems to be caused by a bug in the container runtime `runc 1.1.4` which is fixed in `runc 1.1.10` or newer.
Unfortunately I cannot test if this actually the cause and if upgrading runc actually helps.
What I can for sure is:
The two machines where it works are using either **runc 1.1.0** or **crun 0.17** as container runtime, the failing machine is using runc 1.1.4.

I'll close this issue, because I think that using runc 1.1.10 or newer would solve it.
